title: EliteApply Production Design System
description: A calm, credible and quietly premium design system for scholarship and academic application workflows.

sourceOfTruth.tokens: This JSON file is the machine-readable source of truth for tokens, component contracts and quality gates.
sourceOfTruth.narrative: The companion Markdown file is the human-readable source of truth for rationale, marketing, SEO and usage guidance.
sourceOfTruth.conflictPolicy: Token values in JSON win. Product and content decisions in Markdown win. Any mismatch must fail design-system CI.

brand.name: EliteApply
brand.positioning: A focused application workspace that helps students organise evidence, improve written materials and move each application forward with confidence.
brand.promise: Make the next responsible application step clear without taking ownership away from the student.

principles.name: Responsible clarity
principles.rule: The interface prioritises the next meaningful action, explains consequences and never manufactures urgency.

principles.name: Evidence over spectacle
principles.rule: Trust is earned through transparent states, useful examples, product proof and specific language—not visual effects or unsupported claims.

principles.name: Student control
principles.rule: Users can review, edit, save, export and recover work. AI-assisted output is always presented as editable support.

principles.name: Academic warmth
principles.rule: Editorial typography and generous whitespace create authority without making the product feel institutional or cold.

principles.name: Accessible by default
principles.rule: Keyboard access, visible focus, semantic HTML, non-colour state cues and reduced motion are component requirements, not optional polish.

antiPatterns: Admission guarantees, invented acceptance rates, fake counters or fabricated testimonials.
Gradient text, neon AI glow, glassmorphism, oversized floating icons or repetitive feature-card grids.
Career or recruitment terminology in scholarship and academic application workflows.
Decorative cobalt, coral text on white, or colour-only status communication.
Autosave or AI generation that hides state, failure, conflicts, costs or user control.

usageRules: Coral is decorative or used with dark ink; it is never body text on white and never a routine product action.
Primary cobalt is reserved for links, primary actions, selection, focus and informative state.
Text contrast must be at least 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text.
Interactive boundaries and focus indicators must reach at least 3:1 against adjacent colours.

fontFamilies.interface: "DM Sans", Inter, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif

fontLoading.strategy: self-host preferred; otherwise preconnect to the font origin and use font-display: swap
rules: Preload only the WOFF2 files needed above the fold.
Use metric-compatible fallbacks or size-adjust to reduce layout shift.
Do not load decorative font weights that are not used.
Interactive labels use at least 14px. Twelve-pixel text is limited to non-essential metadata.
Marketing prose is limited to approximately 60–70 characters per line; long-form content to 65–75.
Use sentence case. Avoid all caps except short eyebrow labels with increased letter spacing.
Numerical data in tables may use tabular-nums.
Animate opacity and transform when possible; avoid layout-triggering animation.
No decorative looping animation in the authenticated product.
Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, disable non-essential movement and use instant state changes.

requirements: All functionality is keyboard operable with a logical focus order.
Visible focus uses a 3px primary outline with 2px offset; never remove focus without an equivalent replacement.
Primary pointer targets are at least 44x44 CSS pixels. Dense desktop controls may be 36px only when spacing prevents accidental activation and a 44px touch presentation is used.
Inputs have persistent visible labels. Placeholder text is supplemental, never the only label.
Validation links the field to plain-language error and recovery text using aria-describedby.
Status changes use role=status or an appropriate live region without stealing focus.
Dialogs trap focus, have a labelled title, close with Escape when safe and restore focus to the trigger.
Drag-and-drop always has a keyboard and touch alternative.
Icons that communicate meaning have accessible names; decorative icons are hidden from assistive technology.
Zoom to 200% and reflow at 320 CSS pixels must not cause loss of content or two-dimensional scrolling except for genuinely tabular or board content.

rules: Prefer direct actions: 'Add a deadline' rather than 'Deadline management'.
Explain system state and recovery: what happened, whether work is safe and what the user can do next.
Use 'AI-assisted' only when the feature actually uses AI and immediately explain user control.
Avoid 'guaranteed', 'perfect', 'beat the ATS', 'get accepted' and unsupported superlatives.
Never imply that an application has been reviewed by a university, scholarship provider or admissions expert unless it has.

stateCopy.offline: You are offline. Changes will sync when your connection returns.
stateCopy.conflict: This application changed in another session. Review the latest version before continuing.
stateCopy.generationPending: Preparing a draft from the information you provided…
stateCopy.generationFailed: We could not prepare this draft. Your original content is safe. Try again or continue manually.
stateCopy.emptyApplications: No applications yet. Add one to organise its deadline, requirements and documents.
stateCopy.destructiveConfirm: Delete this application and its workspace? This cannot be undone.

requirements: Loading preserves width, sets aria-busy=true and prevents duplicate submission.
Disabled buttons remain readable and expose a native disabled state.
One primary action per visual region unless a deliberate choice is being presented.
Must have an accessible name
Tooltip may supplement but never replace the accessible name
Link text describes the destination or action; avoid standalone 'Learn more'.
External links are identified when opening a new context.
Links remain distinguishable without relying only on colour in body copy.
Persistent label, optional helper text and explicit error text.
Do not validate on every keystroke unless feedback is genuinely helpful.
Preserve entered values after recoverable errors.
Show character or word guidance only when there is a real limit or recommendation.
Do not block paste, browser spelling tools or standard keyboard shortcuts.
Prefer native select for short, stable lists.
Use a combobox only for searchable lists and implement full keyboard semantics.
Use concise text and a non-colour cue where ambiguity is possible.
Do not use badge colour as the sole stage control.
Include a short title only when it improves scanning.
Blocking errors provide a direct recovery action.
Dismissal is optional and must not hide unresolved blocking information.
Use roving tabindex and arrow-key navigation for true tabs.
Use links instead when each destination has its own URL and page load.
Current state uses text, selected surface or underline—not colour alone.
Active item includes aria-current=page.
Collapsed icon-only items expose labels through accessible names and tooltips.
Mobile drawer has a visible close action, scrim and focus restoration.
Show institution or programme, application type, deadline, stage and one clear next action.
Avoid displaying more than two secondary metadata rows at rest.
The card itself is not a giant link when it contains nested controls.
Stage can be changed with a labelled select or explicit menu; drag-and-drop is enhancement only.

applicationBoard.desktop: Horizontally scrollable columns with sticky column headers and 300px cards.
applicationBoard.mobile: Default to a filterable list grouped by stage; provide board as an optional view.
requirements: Persist the user's selected view.
Announce stage changes and support undo for reversible movement.
Do not trap horizontal scrolling at the page level.
Use for focused decisions, not routine navigation.
Destructive confirmation names the object and consequence.
Primary action appears last in DOM and visually aligns with platform convention.
Never use a toast as the only place for an error that requires action.
Pause dismissal on hover or focus.
Do not stack more than three; consolidate repeated events.
Match final geometry to reduce layout shift.
Use only when content is expected quickly; otherwise show explicit loading copy.
Disable shimmer under reduced motion.
Explain why the area is empty, what value the first item provides and one primary action.
Do not use decorative illustration as a substitute for guidance.
Use semantic table markup and scoped headers.
Provide a mobile alternative or controlled horizontal scroll.
Sorting controls announce direction and are keyboard operable.

marketing.primaryAudience: Students and applicants managing scholarship, university or programme applications.
marketing.secondaryAudience: Advisers, mentors and teams supporting applicants, only where collaboration is actually supported.
messageHierarchy: Outcome: organise stronger applications with less uncertainty.
Mechanism: one workspace for deadlines, requirements, evidence and written materials.
Control: AI-assisted support remains editable and transparent.
Proof: demonstrate real workflows, templates, exports, security and customer evidence that can be verified.

hero.headline: Build stronger applications, without losing your voice.
hero.supportingCopy: Organise deadlines and requirements, shape your written materials and keep every application moving from one focused workspace.
hero.visualDirection: Use a real product composition showing an application checklist, document status and deadline—not a generic AI illustration.

sectionOrder: Trust strip using only verifiable proof
premiumDirection: Use editorial asymmetry, generous negative space, a disciplined baseline grid and one strong product composition.
Alternate white and cool-workspace sections; reserve dark marketing ink for one proof or closing band.
Use coral only as a small waypoint, annotation or editorial marker.
Prefer thin rules, captions, document details and real UI crops over floating 3D objects.
Avoid more than three equal feature cards in one row; use narrative layouts and product-led proof.

allowed: Specific product capabilities verified against the backend and current release.
Customer quotes with documented permission and attribution.
Measured results with sample size, period, method and qualifying context.
prohibited: Guaranteed scholarships, acceptance or admissions outcomes.
Unverifiable user counts, acceptance rates, money won or time saved.
Logos implying customers or partnerships without permission.
Artificial scarcity or countdown timers not tied to a real deadline.

rules: Do not block a URL in robots.txt when relying on a page-level noindex; crawlers must be able to fetch the directive.
Include only canonical, indexable, 200-status public URLs in XML sitemaps.
Use one canonical HTTPS host and redirect all variants.

metadata.defaultDescription: Organise deadlines, requirements and written materials in one focused workspace for scholarship and academic applications.
requirements: Every indexable page has a unique title, meta description, canonical URL and one descriptive H1.
Open Graph and Twitter metadata use a 1200x630 image with readable text-safe margins.
Set language and direction on the HTML element; use hreflang only for fully localised equivalents.

recommendedPublicArchitecture.contentRole: Feature landing page; publish only when the workflow is available
Feature landing page; publish only when the workflow is available
Feature landing page; publish only when the workflow is available
Indexable template hub with unique useful content
Curated example hub; avoid thin programmatic pages
Editorial hub with author, review and update signals

rules: Structured data must match visible page content and current pricing/capabilities.
Use JSON-LD and validate in automated tests plus Google's Rich Results Test where applicable.
Do not add review or rating markup unless ratings are genuine, visible and policy-compliant.

contentQuality: Write for a clear applicant question or decision, not keyword density.
Use expert review, concrete examples, update dates and transparent authorship where appropriate.
Avoid near-duplicate template and location pages with token-swapped copy.
Use descriptive internal links between features, examples, guides and pricing.
Add useful alt text to informative images and empty alt text to decorative images.

performanceBudgets.heroImage: <= 220KB where practical, responsive AVIF/WebP with explicit dimensions
rules: Server-render or statically render indexable marketing content.
Do not hide primary copy behind client-only rendering.
Reserve image dimensions, lazy-load below-fold media and defer non-critical scripts.
Track field data with real-user monitoring, not Lighthouse alone.
Design mobile content order first; do not merely stack desktop columns.
At <=1024px, collapse the permanent sidebar and preserve the current page title and main action.
At <=768px, use a single primary column, 16px gutters and sticky actions only when they do not cover content.
At <=640px, show list view by default for applications and keep form controls full width where practical.
Do not place primary and destructive actions adjacent on narrow screens.
Tables and boards use contained horizontal scrolling with visible affordances, not page-level overflow.

principles: Collect the minimum data needed to improve the product.
Never include essay text, personal statements, document content or sensitive field values in analytics payloads.
Use stable event names and versioned properties.

designSystemCI: JSON parses and all token references resolve.
No raw colour, spacing, radius, shadow or typography values in product components except documented exceptions.
JSON and Markdown version fields match.
Component stories cover all documented states.
automated: axe-core reports no serious or critical violations on representative pages.
Playwright tests keyboard navigation, focus restoration, form errors, dialogs and responsive navigation.
Visual regression tests cover 375, 768, 1024 and 1440 pixel viewports.
Lighthouse CI enforces agreed performance, accessibility, SEO and best-practice thresholds.
Metadata tests verify title, description, canonical, robots and structured data for public routes.
Link checker rejects broken internal links and accidental staging URLs.
manual: Keyboard-only completion of every primary workflow.
Screen-reader smoke test with VoiceOver or NVDA.
Slow network, offline, timeout, expired session, conflict and partial-failure states.
Copy review for unsupported claims, academic terminology and recovery clarity.
releaseBlockers: Missing visible focus, inaccessible primary workflow or colour contrast failure.
Unsupported marketing claim or fabricated proof.
Data loss on recoverable error, retry or session refresh.
Core metadata missing from an indexable route.
Critical layout overflow at supported viewport widths.
